# UC-22.10.42 · Patient Portal — Suspicious MyChart Password Reset & MFA Changes

## Description

Detects takeover patterns on patient portals that could expose ePHI and undermine patient trust and access controls.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes mychart and sourcetypes epic:mychart_audit, epic:mychart_audit`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Mask MRN in non-production; (2) integrate fraud scoring from vendor if available; (3) patient outreach playbook for suspected takeover. Indexes required: `index=mychart`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=epic:mychart_audit`, `sourcetype=epic:mychart_audit``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=mychart` `sourcetype=epic:mychart_audit` (`action`, `user_mrn`, `src_ip`), optional Okta/Azure AD B2C logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Mask MRN in non-production; (2) integrate fraud scoring from vendor if available; (3) patient outreach playbook for suspected takeover.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=mychart sourcetype="epic:mychart_audit" earliest=-7d (action="PasswordReset" OR action="MFAEnrollmentChanged" OR action="EmailChanged")
| stats earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last dc(src_ip) as src_dc values(action) as actions by user_mrn
| where src_dc>=3 OR (last-first)<300 AND mvcount(actions)>=2
| sort - src_dc
```

Understanding this SPL

**Patient Portal — Suspicious MyChart Password Reset & MFA Changes (§164.312(d) / §164.530(c))** — Detects takeover patterns on patient portals that could expose ePHI and undermine patient trust and access controls.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=mychart` `sourcetype=epic:mychart_audit` (`action`, `user_mrn`, `src_ip`), optional Okta/Azure AD B2C logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: mychart; **sourcetype**: epic:mychart_audit. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=mychart, sourcetype="epic:mychart_audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user_mrn** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where src_dc>=3 OR (last-first)<300 AND mvcount(actions)>=2` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Patient Portal — Suspicious MyChart Password Reset & MFA Changes (§164.312(d) / §164.530(c))** — Detects takeover patterns on patient portals that could expose ePHI and undermine patient trust and access controls.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=mychart` `sourcetype=epic:mychart_audit` (`action`, `user_mrn`, `src_ip`), optional Okta/Azure AD B2C logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (MRN hash, actions, IPs), Timeline (burst resets).

## SPL

```spl
index=mychart sourcetype="epic:mychart_audit" earliest=-7d (action="PasswordReset" OR action="MFAEnrollmentChanged" OR action="EmailChanged")
| stats earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last dc(src_ip) as src_dc values(action) as actions by user_mrn
| where src_dc>=3 OR (last-first)<300 AND mvcount(actions)>=2
| sort - src_dc
```

## Visualization

Table (MRN hash, actions, IPs), Timeline (burst resets).
